phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q poke on his last-hits — the poison-into-E pattern starts at level 1 and your DPS already out-trades most mids.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W changes the geometry: miasma GROUNDS dashes. Drop it on his engage path and the lane's mobility advantage evaporates.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: Q-poison into spammable E twin fangs is real sustained damage. Trade anyone who steps into Q range.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Tear stacking quietly while your E heals off poisoned targets — you win every extended trade from here.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Petrifying Gaze online — the all-in answer. Face-stun his engage or slow his retreat; either way the fangs finish it.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Seraph's/Liandry's spike: your DPS is now the highest in the lane, melee-DPS-carry high. Force extended trades on every cooldown gap.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late-game Cass is a hyper-carry: ground their divers, petrify their engage, and machine-gun whoever is closest.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight deficit at level 1: Lux's snare-shield poke combos edge out your grounded-zone DPS on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean his way — the snare-shield poke combos out-tick your grounded-zone DPS in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: Q-poison into spammable E twin fangs is real sustained damage. Trade anyone who steps into Q range.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — grounded-zone DPS edge out the snare-shield poke combos when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight edge at the level-6 breakpoint: your grounded-zone DPS beat Lux's snare-shield poke combos on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your grounded-zone DPS beat Galio's anti-magic shield trades on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — grounded-zone DPS edge out the anti-magic shield trades when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Seraph's/Liandry's spike: your DPS is now the highest in the lane, melee-DPS-carry high. Force extended trades on every cooldown gap.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean your way — grounded-zone DPS edge out the anti-magic shield trades when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q poke on his last-hits — the poison-into-E pattern starts at level 1 and your DPS already out-trades most mids.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight deficit at level 2: Irelia's wave-dash blade stacks edge out your grounded-zone DPS on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean his way — the wave-dash blade stacks out-tick your grounded-zone DPS in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Tear stacking quietly while your E heals off poisoned targets — you win every extended trade from here.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Petrifying Gaze online — the all-in answer. Face-stun his engage or slow his retreat; either way the fangs finish it.
